subject: [AAF] externalizes init data out from aaf-cass image to chart
commit_message:
[AAF] externalizes init data out from aaf-cass image to chart

The initialization data appear as resources to the chart.
The templates were modified to define ConfigMaps and
Deployment to mount them to the aaf-cass container
when it starts.  The existing aaf-cass initialization
script in the image will load the database from these files.

Issue-ID: OOM-2586
Signed-off-by: John J. Franey <john.franey@att.com>
Change-Id: I6be0d4c6739cedfbca5df4ddb527eee2583f74a7
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

You created the config map two times (with same name, just the app label changed) and mounted it 2 times at the same place into the pod.
I believe one is sufficient no?

I'm OK with having them on OOM, I'd just like to have @Mike, @Borislav and @Krzysztof thoughts

>

> I believe one is sufficient no?

> I'm OK with having them on OOM, I'd just like to have @Mike, @Borislav and @Krzysztof thoughts

I'm sorry for the confusion. The difference in the names is nearly invisible, and I didn't write up any description for needing the second ConfigMap.  I don't blame you for calling me out.  😊

During the work, I discovered a second initialization file: identities.dat, and I did not alter the jira ticket to discuss.  This file is not for the database, but had been built into the aaf-cass image for some time.  I suppose I can look into mounting it on other containers would access it.

This second file and the set of database initialization files are in different ConfigMaps, with different names, and mount points.  It is difficult to see the difference in the names (one ends in 's', the other in 'a').  These unfortunate names are used within the image for different directories.  


John

Thanks.

The above implies that the services are not starting.  In testing this change, I looked at the cassandra container only.  The data is the same and I didn't try to startup any of the services before I made this change request.  I didn't anticipate a problem as follows.  This can be related to what you are seeing


When I run the helm templates for aaf services, the containers' image are not correct.   This issue exist on the master branch before my change.  I use 'helm template --debug  aaf/components/aaf-locate | less'

From the output, in Deployment for the locate service (as example), I see this:

      containers:
      - name: aaf-locate
        workingDir: /opt/app/aaf
        command: ["bin/locate"]
        image: nexus3.onap.org:10001/
        imagePullPolicy: Always

The string for image is wrong.  It should be similar to the one like for aaf-config container:
     - name: aaf-locate-config-container
        image: nexus3.onap.org:10001/onap/aaf/aaf_config:2.1.23


This template issue is recreated in the parent commit of my change.   I think perhaps something is different with the common templates which require a value  which aaf isn't setting now?

What is fix?
